basicDetails.schemeName: Logistics Excellence, Advancement, and Performance Shield

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The **Logistics Excellence, Advancement, and Performance Shield** (LEAPS) scheme under the **Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India,** is a flagship initiative designed to acknowledge and celebrate best practices and innovations within the logistics industry. In line with the National Logistics Policy 2022, LEAPS aims to create a new benchmark in India as well as globally by recognizing outstanding leadership and innovative endeavours across various sectors of the logistics industry, including air, road, sea, rail freight, warehousing, and multimodal transportation. It also recognizes MSMEs, startups, and academia that provide innovative solutions across the logistics ecosystem. LEAPS also raises awareness about sustainability, ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) practices, and green logistics, while promoting collaboration between government, industry, and academia to drive progress and efficiency. Building upon the foundation laid by the PM GatiShakti National Master Plan and the National Logistics Policy, LEAPS endeavours to further enhance India’s logistics infrastructure and efficiency. It aims to create a technologically enabled, integrated, and sustainable logistics ecosystem. This is the 4th edition of LEAPS. > **The key guiding principles for “LEAPS” are:** 1. Comprehensive coverage of all logistics sectors and ecosystem enablers 1. Special focus on MSMEs and Startups 1. Showcase of diversity, inclusion and ESG best practices in the logistics industry 1. Alignment with the objectives of PM Gati Shakti 1. Alignment with the mission of Aatmanirbhar Bharat 1. Process innovation, efficiency aided by technology adoption.
schemeContent.exclusions_md: **Note (Applicable to all Category):** The entity should be in operations for at least 3 financial years. For FY 2024-25, provisional financial statement may be considered.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to acknowledge and celebrate best practices and innovations in the logistics industry across sectors. It also aims to promote sustainability, efficiency, and technological advancement in alignment with national logistics initiatives.

children.text: Logistics Excellence, Advancement, and Performance Shield
Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India,

is a flagship initiative designed to acknowledge and celebrate best practices and innovations within the logistics industry. In line with the National Logistics Policy 2022, LEAPS aims to create a new benchmark in India as well as globally by recognizing outstanding leadership and innovative endeavours across various sectors of the logistics industry, including air, road, sea, rail freight, warehousing, and multimodal transportation. It also recognizes MSMEs, startups, and academia that provide innovative solutions across the logistics ecosystem.

LEAPS also raises awareness about sustainability, ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) practices, and green logistics, while promoting collaboration between government, industry, and academia to drive progress and efficiency. Building upon the foundation laid by the PM GatiShakti National Master Plan and the National Logistics Policy, LEAPS endeavours to further enhance India’s logistics infrastructure and efficiency. It aims to create a technologically enabled, integrated, and sustainable logistics ecosystem. This is the 4th edition of LEAPS.
The key guiding principles for “LEAPS” are:
Comprehensive coverage of all logistics sectors and ecosystem enablers
Special focus on MSMEs and Startups
Showcase of diversity, inclusion and ESG best practices in the logistics industry
Alignment with the objectives of PM Gati Shakti
Alignment with the mission of Aatmanirbhar Bharat
Process innovation, efficiency aided by technology adoption.
The entity should be in operations for at least 3 financial years. For FY 2024-25, provisional financial statement may be considered.

- Name as per Aadhaar/Valid Identity Proof.

- Brief Description about the Organization (100 Words)

applicationProcess.process_md: **Registration:** **Step-1:** Visit the [official website](https://awards.gov.in/). **Step-2:** Click on the [Registration ](https://awards.gov.in/Registration/SignUp)button. **Step-3:** Choose type Individual or Organization. **Step-4.1:** Under Individual category provide the following details. - Name as per Aadhaar/Valid Identity Proof. - Date of Birth. - Nominator/Applicant Type. - Mobile No. - Email ID. - Aadhaar Number. **Step-4.2:** Under Organization category provide the following details. - Type of Organization. - Name of the Authorized Person as per Aadhaar/Valid Identity Proof - Date of Birth. - Brief Description about the Organization (100 Words) - Designation - Mobile No. - Email ID. - Aadhaar Number. **Step-5:** Submit. **Apply:** **Step-1:** Visit the [official website](https://awards.gov.in/). **Step-2:** Click on l[ogin button on](https://awards.gov.in/Login) portal and login using the credentials. **Step-3:** From the list of awards, select scheme name, then click ["Nominate / Apply Now".](https://awards.gov.in/Home/AwardLibrary) **Step-4:** On the next page, fill in all the mandatory fields in the online application form, and then click "Submit".

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: **A. Core logistics and Special categories** 1. The applicant must be a company registered in India under the Companies Act of 2013 or a partnership firm registered under the Limited Liability Partnership Act of 2008. 2. The entity must possess all relevant trade-specific registrations, such as GST Registration, etc. 3. The entity, its promoters, or any of their affiliated entities must not have incurred any defaults in the last three fiscal years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25). 4. The entity must submit audited financial statements (balance sheet, profit & loss account) for the last three financial years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25). **B. Startups** 1. The startup should be a DPIIT recognized startup. The entity must submit their certificate of incorporation or partnership deed. 2. The entity must have a hardware or software product or a process solution that is present in the market. 3. The entity must have all applicable trade or trade-specific registrations (Example: FSSAI, MSME, GST Registration, etc.) 4. The entity, its promoters, or any of their affiliated entities must not have incurred any defaults in the last three fiscal years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25). 5. The entity must submit audited financial statements (balance sheet, profit & loss account) for the last three financial years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25). **C. MSMEs** 1. The entity should be a recognized MSME entity (Udyam Aadhar). The entity must submit their certificate of incorporation or partnership deed. 2. The entity must have all applicable trade or trade-specific registrations (example: MSME, GST Registration, etc.) 3. The entity, its promoters, or any of their affiliated entities must not have incurred any defaults in the last three fiscal years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25). 4. The entity must submit audited financial statements (balance sheet, profit & loss account) for the last three financial years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25). **D. Institutions:** 1. Institution must also be officially registered with the appropriate educational or governmental authorities. 2. This should include accreditation from national or regional educational bodies.

children.text: A. Core logistics and Special categories
1. The applicant must be a company registered in India under the Companies Act of 2013 or a partnership firm registered under the Limited Liability Partnership Act of 2008.
2. The entity must possess all relevant trade-specific registrations, such as GST Registration, etc.
3. The entity, its promoters, or any of their affiliated entities must not have incurred any defaults in the last three fiscal years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25).
4. The entity must submit audited financial statements (balance sheet, profit & loss account) for the last three financial years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25).
1. The startup should be a DPIIT recognized startup. The entity must submit their certificate of incorporation or partnership deed.
2. The entity must have a hardware or software product or a process solution that is present in the market.
3. The entity must have all applicable trade or trade-specific registrations (Example: FSSAI, MSME, GST Registration, etc.)
4. The entity, its promoters, or any of their affiliated entities must not have incurred any defaults in the last three fiscal years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25).
5. The entity must submit audited financial statements (balance sheet, profit & loss account) for the last three financial years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25).
1. The entity should be a recognized MSME entity (Udyam Aadhar). The entity must submit their certificate of incorporation or partnership deed.
2. The entity must have all applicable trade or trade-specific registrations (example: MSME, GST Registration, etc.)
3. The entity, its promoters, or any of their affiliated entities must not have incurred any defaults in the last three fiscal years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25).
4. The entity must submit audited financial statements (balance sheet, profit & loss account) for the last three financial years (FY 2022-23, 23-24, 24-25).
1. Institution must also be officially registered with the appropriate educational or governmental authorities.
2. This should include accreditation from national or regional educational bodies.
